description: Deep-fried pieces of dough, usually light and airy, most famously served hot with a heavy dusting of powdered sugar. New Orleans-style beignets are typically square and yeast-leavened.
core_ingredients: Yeast or choux-style dough base depending on style
preparation_steps: Prepare the dough and let it rise if making a yeast-based version.
Roll and cut into pieces, often squares for New Orleans style.
Drain briefly and dust generously with powdered sugar.

health_advice.benefits: Mainly a pleasure food rather than a nutrient-dense food.
health_advice.risks: Deep frying and added sugar make it energy-dense.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Best enjoyed occasionally and soon after cooking, in small portions.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are vegetarian. Vegan versions omit dairy and eggs. Standard versions are not gluten-free.

allergens: Dairy if milk or butter is used
Egg if egg-enriched dough is used
